basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/Amazon%20Web%20Services/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The Amazon Web Services solution for Microsoft Sentinel allows you to enable Security monitoring of AWS services by allowing ingestion of logs from the AWS CloudTrail platform, VPC Flow Logs, AWS GuardDuty and AWS CloudWatch. **Data Connectors:** 3, **Workbooks:** 2, **Analytic Rules:** 62, **Hunting Queries:** 36 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

metadata.hidden: Hiding location, we get it from the log analytics workspace

basics.name: getLAWorkspace
basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for Amazon Web Services. You can get Amazon Web Services custom log data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

This Solution installs the data connector for Amazon Web Services. You can get Amazon Web Services custom log data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

This Solution installs the data connector for Amazon Web Services S3 WAF. You can get Amazon Web Services S3 WAF data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This solution installs workbook(s) to help you gain insights into the telemetry collected in Microsoft Sentinel. After installing the solution, start using the workbook in Manage solution view.

elements.name: workbook1
elements.label: AWS Network Activities

options.text: Gain insights into AWS network related resource activities, including the creation, update, and deletions of security groups, network ACLs and routes, gateways, elastic load balancers, VPCs, subnets, and network interfaces.

elements.name: workbook2
elements.label: AWS User Activities

options.text: Gain insights into AWS user activities, including failed sign-in attempts, IP addresses, regions, user agents, and identity types, as well as potential malicious user activities with assumed roles.
This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Changes to internet facing AWS RDS Database instances

options.text:
Identifies AWS CloudTrail events associated with changes to Amazon RDS database security groups and database
security group ingress rules, which may indicate unauthorized modification of internet-facing RDS access. Validate whether
the change was authorized and consistent with change control policy.
RDS API Reference Docs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Changes to Amazon VPC settings

options.text:
Identifies changes to Amazon VPC(Virtual Private Cloud) settings that can alter network exposure, routing, or access controls.
This rule identifies changes to Amazon VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) settings such as new ACL entries, routes, routetable or Gateways. 
AWS VPC API Docs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/OperationList-query-vpc.html'

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Changes made to AWS CloudTrail logs

options.text:
Identifies manipulation of AWS CloudTrail, CloudWatch/EventBridge, or VPC Flow Logs that can indicate defense evasion.
This alert identifies any manipulation of AWS CloudTrail, Cloudwatch/EventBridge or VPC Flow logs.
More Information: AWS CloudTrail API: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html 
AWS Cloudwatch/Eventbridge API: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html 
AWS DelteteFlowLogs API : https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_DeleteFlowLogs.html '

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Config Service Resource Deletion Attempts

options.text: Identifies AWS API calls that attempt to reduce logging or visibility in an account by stopping logging, deleting trails, deleting flow logs, or deleting event buses. This behavior can indicate defense evasion or the deliberate suppression of telemetry used to monitor security posture.

elements.name: analytic5
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Login to AWS Management Console without MFA

options.text:
Identifies successful AWS Management Console sign-ins where CloudTrail records a ConsoleLogin event without
multi-factor authentication. The rule looks for logins where MFAUsed is not Yes and the console response is not
Failure, which can indicate credential misuse or weak account protection.

elements.name: analytic6
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Monitor AWS Credential abuse or hijacking

options.text:
Identifies AWS STS `GetCallerIdentity` calls made by assumed-role sessions.
An attacker who has assumed the role of a legitimate account can call the GetCallerIdentity function to determine what account they are using.
A legitimate user using legitimate credentials would not need to call GetCallerIdentity since they should already know what account they are using.
More Information: https://duo.com/decipher/trailblazer-hunts-compromised-credentials-in-aws 
AWS STS GetCallerIdentity API: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/API_GetCallerIdentity.html

elements.name: analytic7
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Full Admin policy created and then attached to Roles, Users or Groups

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant full administrative access and subsequent attachment to a role, user, or group. This sequence can be used to elevate a low-privilege identity to administrative access and should be investigated immediately.
AWS IAM Policy Grammar: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/reference_policies_grammar.html 
and AWS IAM API at https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html

elements.name: analytic8
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Changes to AWS Security Group ingress and egress settings

options.text:
A Security Group acts as a virtual firewall for an AWS instance to control inbound and outbound traffic. This rule detects AWS CloudTrail events for changes to Security Group ingress and egress settings.
Investigate to validate the legitimacy of the activity and identify potential malicious activity.

elements.name: analytic9
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Changes to AWS Elastic Load Balancer security groups

options.text:
Detects AWS CloudTrail events that modify Elastic Load Balancer security group associations. Unauthorized
changes can expose services to unintended network access or weaken cloud boundary protections. Validate these
updates against approved change control and deployment activity.

elements.name: analytic10
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - NRT Login to AWS Management Console without MFA

options.text:
Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) helps you to prevent credential compromise. This alert identifies logins to the AWS Management Console without MFA.
You can limit this detection to trigger for administrative accounts if you do not have MFA enabled on all accounts.
This is done by looking at the eventName ConsoleLogin and if the AdditionalEventData field indicates MFA was NOT used and the ResponseElements field indicates NOT a Failure. Thereby indicating that a non-MFA login was successful.

elements.name: analytic11
elements.label: AWSGuardDuty - GuardDuty Alert

options.text: Identifies Amazon GuardDuty findings and creates a Microsoft Sentinel alert for each finding. Use the GuardDuty finding details in the alert to determine the specific malicious or suspicious activity that was detected.

elements.name: analytic12
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - ECR image scan findings high or critical

options.text: Identifies Amazon ECR image scan findings that report high or critical severity vulnerabilities. These findings indicate container images that should be reviewed and remediated before deployment or continued use.

elements.name: analytic13
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Suspicious command sent to EC2

options.text: An attacker with the necessary AWS permissions could be executing code remotely on an EC2 instance via SSM and saving the output to their own S3 bucket. Verify this action with the user identity and confirm it was authorized.

elements.name: analytic14
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Successful API executed from a Tor exit node

options.text:
Identifies successful AWS CloudTrail API activity originating from an IP address identified as a TOR exit node in
the external TOR list hosted at https://firewalliplists.gypthecat.com/lists/kusto/kusto-tor-exit.csv.zip. The rule alerts
only when CloudTrail indicates the request completed successfully with no ErrorCode and no ErrorMessage, and the source
IP is present in the TOR exit node list.

elements.name: analytic15
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - AWS GuardDuty detector disabled or suspended

options.text: Identifies AWS GuardDuty detectors being disabled or suspended. This behavior can indicate defense evasion and should be validated with the initiating identity and approved administrative activity.

elements.name: analytic16
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - CloudFormation policy created then used for privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant CloudFormation and IAM permissions commonly used for privilege
escalation, followed by attachment activity to a user, role, or group. Review whether the new policy and any
subsequent attachment were authorized because this pattern can enable an attacker to create or elevate access.

elements.name: analytic17
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of CRUD DynamoDB policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that provide broad CRUD permissions for DynamoDB, followed by policy attachment activity. This sequence can indicate an attempt to grant capabilities that support
privilege escalation and should be validated against approved administrative changes.

elements.name: analytic18
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of new CRUD IAM policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad IAM CRUD permissions, followed by attachment of that policy to a user, role, or group. This pattern may indicate an attempt to establish or expand
privileged access and should be reviewed as potential privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic19
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of CRUD KMS policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant permissions for broad KMS CRUD actions, followed by attachment activity to a principal. This sequence can be used to expand access to encryption keys or
disable protections and should be investigated as possible privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic20
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Created CRUD S3 policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad Amazon S3 CRUD permissions, followed by policy attachment activity to users, roles, or groups. This sequence can indicate attempts to
expand access and should be investigated as potential cloud privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic21
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of CRUD Lambda policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad AWS Lambda CRUD permissions and subsequent policy attachment
activity to a principal. This behavior may indicate an attempt to add capabilities that support privilege
escalation or unauthorized execution pathways.

elements.name: analytic22
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of DataPipeline policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that combine AWS Data Pipeline permissions with IAM privileges such as
iam:PassRole, followed by attachment activity to users, roles, or groups. This sequence can enable escalation
paths and should be reviewed for unauthorized privilege assignment.

elements.name: analytic23
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of EC2 policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad EC2 permissions or EC2 actions combined with IAM pass-role
capability, followed by policy attachment activity. This pattern can indicate attempts to gain elevated cloud
execution privileges and should be validated as authorized change activity.

elements.name: analytic24
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of Glue policy and then privilege escalation

options.text:
Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad AWS Glue permissions paired with IAM
permissions, followed by policy attachment to a principal. This sequence can indicate unauthorized privilege
expansion and should be reviewed for potential cloud account manipulation.

elements.name: analytic25
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of Lambda policy and then privilege escalation

options.text: Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad AWS Lambda permissions followed by attachment to a user, role, or group. This sequence can indicate an attempt to add execution capabilities that support privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic26
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of SSM policy and then privilege escalation

options.text: Identifies creation of IAM policies that grant broad AWS Systems Manager permissions followed by attachment to a user, role, or group. This sequence can indicate an attempt to gain administration capabilities that support privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic27
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creating keys with encrypt policy without MFA

options.text: Identifies AWS KMS key creation or policy changes that allow `kms:Encrypt` or `kms:*` to any principal. This configuration can expose encryption capability outside approved boundaries and should be investigated as overly permissive or malicious.

elements.name: analytic28
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - EC2 Startup Shell Script Changed

options.text: Identifies changes to the EC2 startup script. The shell script will be executed as root/SYSTEM every time the specific instances are booted up. ref : https://github.com/RhinoSecurityLabs/pacu/blob/master/pacu/modules/ec2__startup_shell_script/main.py

elements.name: analytic29
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 Object Exfiltration from Anonymous User

options.text:
Detects S3 GetObject activity performed by anonymous principals, which can indicate unauthorized object access
and potential data exfiltration from public or misconfigured buckets.

elements.name: analytic30
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Amazon ECR image scanning disabled

options.text: Identifies Amazon ECR image scanning being disabled. This change can reduce visibility into vulnerable container images and may indicate defense evasion or weakening of container security controls.

elements.name: analytic31
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Tampering to AWS CloudTrail logs

options.text:
Detects successful attempts to disable, delete, or weaken AWS logging telemetry, including CloudTrail,
CloudWatch/EventBridge, and VPC flow logs. This behavior can indicate defense evasion and deliberate reduction
of incident visibility by an attacker.

elements.name: analytic32
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Network ACL with all the open ports to a specified CIDR

options.text:
Detects Network ACL entries that allow very broad inbound access ranges, including all protocols or large port
ranges. Such changes can significantly increase exposure and may indicate weakening of cloud network controls.

elements.name: analytic33
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Suspicious overly permissive KMS key policy created

options.text:
Detects creation or update of KMS key policies that grant broad encryption permissions to all principals.
Overly permissive key policies can be abused for malicious encryption operations and indicate potential account
compromise or risky misconfiguration.

elements.name: analytic34
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation with AdministratorAccess managed policy

options.text:
Detects successful attachment of the AWS managed AdministratorAccess policy to users, roles, or groups.
This action can immediately grant full administrative permissions and is a high-risk privilege escalation pattern
that should be validated against approved administrative activity.

elements.name: analytic35
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation with admin managed policy

options.text:
Detects successful attachment of admin-related managed IAM policies to users, roles, or groups, excluding
the dedicated AdministratorAccess and FullAccess patterns handled by other detections. This behavior may indicate
unauthorized privilege escalation and should be validated against approved administrative changes.

elements.name: analytic36
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation with FullAccess managed policy

options.text:
Detects successful attachment of AWS managed policies containing FullAccess permissions to users, roles, or
groups, excluding admin-named policies. This action can rapidly expand privilege scope and should be reviewed
as potential cloud privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic37
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via CloudFormation policy

options.text:
Detects creation of inline IAM policies with CloudFormation and IAM permissions associated with privilege
escalation pathways, including iam:PassRole plus stack creation capabilities. This pattern can enable indirect
elevation and should be investigated for unauthorized policy changes.

elements.name: analytic38
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via CRUD DynamoDB policy

options.text:
Detects creation of inline IAM policies that grant broad DynamoDB create, read, update, and delete permissions.
Attackers may use this access expansion to manipulate data stores and escalate influence across cloud workloads.

elements.name: analytic39
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via CRUD IAM policy

options.text:
Detects creation of inline IAM policies that grant broad IAM create, read, update, and delete capabilities.
This behavior can establish or expand privileged control paths in the account and should be reviewed as potential
privilege escalation.

elements.name: analytic40
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via CRUD KMS policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad KMS create, read, update, enable/disable, and delete
permissions. This pattern can support key management abuse and indicates possible privilege escalation through
expanded cloud role capabilities.

elements.name: analytic41
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via CRUD Lambda policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad AWS Lambda lifecycle permissions and related destructive
capabilities. This behavior can establish execution control paths and may indicate privilege escalation through
unauthorized policy expansion.

elements.name: analytic42
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via CRUD S3 policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad Amazon S3 create, read, update, and delete permissions.
This type of policy expansion can be used to gain higher-impact access to cloud data and facilitate privilege
escalation objectives.

elements.name: analytic43
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via DataPipeline policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that combine Data Pipeline permissions with IAM privileges such as
iam:PassRole. This sequence can enable indirect execution and elevated access pathways, indicating potential
privilege escalation activity.

elements.name: analytic44
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via EC2 policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad EC2 permissions or EC2 actions paired with IAM pass-role
capability. This policy pattern can enable launch and control of compute resources as a privilege escalation path.

elements.name: analytic45
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via Glue policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad AWS Glue permissions or Glue actions paired with IAM
privileges such as iam:PassRole. This pattern can enable elevated execution paths and indicates potential
privilege escalation through expanded cloud role capabilities.

elements.name: analytic46
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via Lambda policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad AWS Lambda permissions, including combinations that can
link Lambda with other services such as DynamoDB. These permissions can provide execution control and support
privilege escalation paths in the cloud environment.

elements.name: analytic47
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Privilege escalation via SSM policy

options.text:
Detects inline IAM policy updates that grant broad AWS Systems Manager permissions. This can enable command
execution and control actions across managed instances, representing a potential privilege escalation route.

elements.name: analytic48
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - RDS instance publicly exposed

options.text:
Detects RDS instance creation or modification events where the database is configured as publicly accessible.
Public exposure of database instances can increase the risk of unauthorized access and data leakage.

elements.name: analytic49
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Successful brute force attack on S3 Bucket

options.text:
Detects repeated failed GetObject attempts against an S3 bucket followed by a successful access from the
same source, which can indicate brute-force-style object discovery or access attempts.

elements.name: analytic50
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 bucket access point publicly exposed

options.text:
Detects S3 access point policy changes that allow public principals. Public exposure through access point
configuration can lead to unauthorized data access and potential information disclosure.

elements.name: analytic51
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 bucket exposed via ACL

options.text:
Detects S3 bucket ACL changes that grant public or broadly authenticated access. Overly permissive ACL
assignments can expose bucket contents to unauthorized users.

elements.name: analytic52
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 bucket exposed via policy

options.text:
Detects S3 bucket policy updates that allow public principals without restrictive conditions.
Public bucket policies can expose sensitive data and should be reviewed immediately.

elements.name: analytic53
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 object publicly exposed

options.text:
Detects S3 object ACL changes that expose objects to public or broadly authenticated users.
This can result in unintended data disclosure from object-level permissions.

elements.name: analytic54
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 bucket suspicious ransomware activity

options.text:
Detects a ransomware-like sequence where objects are read from an S3 bucket and then overwritten using an
external KMS key. This pattern can indicate malicious encryption and potential data denial in the bucket.

elements.name: analytic55
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - SAML update identity provider

options.text:
Detects updates to AWS SAML identity provider configuration. Unauthorized changes can allow adversaries to
issue trusted federation tokens and gain persistent or privileged access.

elements.name: analytic56
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Policy version set to default

options.text:
Detects SetDefaultPolicyVersion activity that makes a different IAM policy version active.
Attackers can use this to activate previously created permissive versions and escalate access.

elements.name: analytic57
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - SSM document is publicly exposed

options.text: Detects an AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document that has been made publicly accessible, which could lead to sensitive information exposure. Verify the document configurations and confirm the change was authorized.

elements.name: analytic58
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Suspicious AWS CLI Command Execution

options.text: This detection focuses on identifying potentially suspicious activities involving the execution of AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) commands, particularly focusing on reconnaissance operations.

elements.name: analytic59
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Suspicious AWS EC2 Compute Resource Deployments

options.text: This detection focused on Suspicious deployment of AWS EC2 resource (virtual machine) scale sets was detected. This behavior might indicate that the threat actor is deploying computing resources for cryptocurrency mining activities.This detection centers around identifying suspicious instances of AWS EC2 resource deployment, particularly scale sets. Such behavior raises concerns of potential threat actor involvement, potentially indicative of efforts to deploy computing resources for the purpose of cryptocurrency mining activities

elements.name: analytic60
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Creation of Access Key for IAM User

options.text: Detects creation of a new IAM access key on an existing user account, which could be used to establish persistence. This action should be validated by the AWS account administrator. Reference: https://stratus-red-team.cloud/attack-techniques/AWS/aws.persistence.iam-backdoor-user/

elements.name: analytic61
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - User IAM Enumeration

options.text: Detects enumeration of IAM account configuration via repeated API calls to list roles, users, groups, and policies within a short time period. The threshold can be adjusted to reduce false positives from authorized cloud scanners such as Wiz.

elements.name: analytic62
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Unauthorized EC2 Instance Setup Attempt

options.text: Detects an unauthorized attempt to launch an EC2 instance by a user without the necessary permissions. This may indicate an attempt to provision malicious infrastructure within the AWS subscription. Reference: https://stratus-red-team.cloud/attack-techniques/AWS/aws.execution.ec2-launch-unusual-instances/
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM Policy Change Activity

options.text: Identifies IAM policy modification events that may indicate unauthorized privilege changes, policy tampering, or persistence attempts in AWS accounts. This hunting query depends on AWS AWSS3 data connector (AWSCloudTrail AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery2
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM Privilege Escalation by Instance Profile Attachment

options.text:
Identifies RemoveRoleFromInstanceProfile followed by AddRoleToInstanceProfile from the same source and identity, which may indicate privilege escalation by attaching a higher-privileged role to an instance profile.
AWS Instance Profile: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_use_switch-role-ec2_instance-profiles.html This hunting query depends on AWS AWSS3 data connector (AWSCloudTrail AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery3
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM Privileged Role Attached to Instance

options.text: Identifies when a privileged IAM role is attached to an existing or new instance, which may indicate privilege escalation to administrative level. This hunting query depends on AWS AWSS3 data connector (AWSCloudTrail AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery4
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM suspicious STS AssumeRole from unseen identity

options.text: Identifies STS AssumeRole operations for IAM roles not historically seen from the calling identity, which may indicate credential abuse or lateral movement. This hunting query depends on AWS AWSS3 data connector (AWSCloudTrail AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery5
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Activity in unused or unsupported cloud regions

options.text: Identifies AWS API activity in regions not historically used by the account, which may indicate defense evasion or unauthorized access from a compromised identity. This hunting query depends on AWS AWSS3 data connector (AWSCloudTrail AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery6
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - EC2 Instance Launched Without Key Pair

options.text: Detects EC2 RunInstances events without a key pair and with user data present, which may indicate malicious startup script execution on newly provisioned instances. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery7
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM Assume Role Brute Force

options.text: Identify repeated failed AssumeRole attempts against existing IAM roles, which may indicate privilege escalation or lateral movement using a compromised identity. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery8
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - S3 Bucket Versioning Suspended

options.text: Detects S3 bucket versioning suspension events that may indicate preparation for destructive actions such as ransomware attack by reducing object recovery options. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery9
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM New Access Key Created for User

options.text: Detects CreateAccessKey events where a principal creates new access keys, which may indicate persistence or privilege abuse if generated for another identity. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery10
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM CreateLoginProfile Activity

options.text: Detects CreateLoginProfile events that may indicate unauthorized password creation for IAM users and potential account persistence or privilege abuse. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery11
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - ECR Container Image Low Severity Findings

options.text: Identifies ECR container image scan results reporting low severity vulnerabilities, which may indicate security weaknesses in container images requiring review before deployment. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery12
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - ECR Container Image Medium Severity Findings

options.text: Identifies ECR container image scan results reporting medium severity vulnerabilities, which may indicate security weaknesses in container images requiring prompt review and remediation. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery13
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - High Volume of Enumeration Events

options.text: Identifies identities executing an unusually high volume of enumeration API calls, which may indicate an attacker probing IAM permissions or mapping cloud infrastructure after compromise. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery14
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Failed Brute Force on S3 Bucket

options.text: Identifies repeated failed S3 GetObject attempts from anonymous or mismatched identities, which may indicate brute force activity against bucket objects. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery15
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Multiple Failed Login Attempts Without MFA

options.text: Identifies repeated AWS Console login failures for identities without MFA, which may indicate password brute force activity against user accounts. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery16
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM AccessDenied discovery events

options.text: Identifies excessive AccessDenied discovery events for IAM users within short intervals, which may indicate stolen credentials being used to enumerate permissions and resources. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery17
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM user and group object changes

options.text: Identifies IAM user and group object creation or deletion events that may indicate unauthorized identity administration, privilege abuse, or defense evasion activity. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery18
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Lambda function throttled

options.text: Identifies Lambda function concurrency reservation set to zero, which attackers may use for denial of service. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery19
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Lambda layer imported from external account

options.text: Identifies Lambda layer imports from external AWS accounts, which may indicate backdoor injection for code execution during function invocation. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery20
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Lambda function code updated

options.text: Identifies IAM users updating Lambda function code via UpdateFunctionCode, which may indicate unauthorized code injection or persistence mechanism deployment. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery21
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - IAM login profile updated

options.text: Identifies UpdateLoginProfile activity that may indicate unauthorized password changes used for persistence or privilege escalation. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery22
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - Route table attribute modifications

options.text: Identifies route table modifications that may indicate attempts to redirect traffic or gain unauthorized access to network resources. This hunting query depends on AWS data connector (AWSCloudTrail Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery23

elements.name: huntingquery32
elements.label: AWSCloudTrail - AWS STS token suspicious activity from EC2

options.text: Suspicious activity of the STS token of an EC2 machine hosted by ECS (for example, by SSRF) indicates a possible token hijacking.
